Use_Case: Baseline and outcome tracking for depression protocols
Notes: Universal baseline for TMS/neurofeedback depression. Cutoff ≥10 for moderate.

Use_Case: Research outcome measure for depression protocols
Notes: Cognitive/affective/somatic subscales. More sensitive to cognitive features than PHQ-9.

Use_Case: Gold standard clinician-rated outcome for TMS trials
Notes: Used in FDA clearance trials. Less practical for routine clinical use than PHQ-9.

Use_Case: Change-sensitive clinician-rated depression outcome
License_or_Access_Notes: Free for clinical/research use; commercial adaptation requires permission
Notes: Superior sensitivity to change during neuromodulation. Preferred in European TMS trials.

Use_Case: Brief self-report tracking between sessions
Notes: Used in STAR*D trial. Covers all DSM-5 MDD symptom domains.

Use_Case: Baseline and outcome tracking for anxiety
Related_Conditions: GAD; Panic; Social anxiety; PTSD screen
Notes: Paired with PHQ-9 as universal neuromodulation baseline.

Use_Case: Discriminate anxiety from depression in research
Notes: Discriminates between anxiety and depression when both present.

Use_Case: Clinician-rated anxiety outcome for TMS trials
Notes: Psychic subscale relevant for neurofeedback frontal alpha protocols.

Use_Case: Distinguish state vs trait anxiety in treatment
Notes: State subscale useful for pre/post individual session assessment.

Use_Case: ADHD screening and outcome tracking in adults
Notes: Part A (6 items) for quick repeated screening.

Use_Case: Comprehensive ADHD assessment with multi-informant forms
Notes: Multi-informant tracking during neurofeedback.

Use_Case: Parent/teacher-rated ADHD tracking in pediatric NF studies
License_or_Access_Notes: Free for clinical and research use
Notes: Frequently used in pediatric neurofeedback research.

Use_Case: Real-world executive function tracking
Notes: Captures real-world EF behavior missed by cognitive tests.

Use_Case: Insomnia tracking for CES/neurofeedback sleep protocols
License_or_Access_Notes: Free with attribution; request via ePROVIDE
Notes: Cutoff ≥10 for subthreshold; ≥15 for clinical insomnia.

Use_Case: Comprehensive 1-month sleep quality assessment
License_or_Access_Notes: Available with permission from University of Pittsburgh
Related_Conditions: Sleep disorders; Depression; Chronic pain; PTSD
Notes: Broader than ISI; includes sleep architecture components.

Use_Case: Screen for excessive daytime sleepiness at intake
License_or_Access_Notes: Copyright — license required (Mapi Research Trust); academic waiver available
Notes: Safety screen: severe EDS may indicate untreated sleep apnea.

Use_Case: Daily prospective sleep tracking during treatment
License_or_Access_Notes: Free — open-access; Consensus Sleep Diary format
Scoring_Type: Daily entries; derived SOL, WASO, SE%, TST
Notes: More sensitive to within-week changes than retrospective measures.

Use_Case: Pre/post-session pain rating in neuromodulation
Notes: Universal pain intensity measure. FDA-accepted PRO.; Public domain — no single authoritative URL

Use_Case: Verbal pain rating during treatment sessions
Notes: IMMPACT consensus core outcome. Higher compliance than VAS clinically.; Public domain — no single authoritative URL

Use_Case: Comprehensive pain severity and interference tracking
License_or_Access_Notes: Free for academic research with attribution (MD Anderson)
Notes: IMMPACT-recommended. Captures both intensity and functional interference.

Use_Case: Multidimensional pain quality assessment
License_or_Access_Notes: Copyright — Mapi Research Trust; academic use often free
Notes: Neuropathic quality subscale valuable for mechanism-specific neuromodulation.

Use_Case: Cognitive screening for neuromodulation patients
License_or_Access_Notes: Free for clinical use; certification required since 2020
Notes: Quick 10-min screen. Certification required for use since 2020.

Assessment_Name: Trail Making Test (Parts A and B)
Use_Case: Processing speed and cognitive flexibility assessment
Notes: Part A: processing speed. Part B: cognitive flexibility/switching.; Public domain — no single authoritative URL

Use_Case: Inhibitory control assessment
License_or_Access_Notes: Public domain (basic versions); PAR standardized version requires purchase
Notes: Measures prefrontal inhibitory control.

Use_Case: PTSD symptom tracking for neuromodulation protocols
Notes: Cutoff 31-33 for probable PTSD. Cluster scores available.

Use_Case: Gold standard clinician-rated PTSD assessment
License_or_Access_Notes: Free — request form from VA/NCPTSD; training recommended
Notes: Gold standard for PTSD diagnosis and severity. Training recommended.

Use_Case: OCD severity tracking for Deep TMS / rTMS protocols
Notes: Primary outcome in OCD neuromodulation trials. ≥30% reduction = response.

Use_Case: Clinician-rated global severity and improvement tracking
Notes: Quick clinician gestalt. CGI-I useful for treatment response.; Public domain — no single authoritative URL

Use_Case: Functional disability tracking across conditions
Notes: Condition-agnostic. Useful across neuromodulation protocols.

Assessment_Name: TMS Safety Screening Questionnaire (Rossi et al.)
Use_Case: Pre-treatment safety screening for all TMS patients
License_or_Access_Notes: Free — published as part of safety guidelines
Notes: MANDATORY before TMS. Covers metallic implants, seizure history, medications, pregnancy.

Assessment_Name: tDCS Adverse Effects Questionnaire (Brunoni et al.)
Use_Case: Systematic AE tracking during tDCS treatment
Scoring_Type: Systematic symptom checklist with severity ratings
Notes: Tracks: tingling, itching, burning, headache, scalp pain, etc.

Use_Case: Universal contraindication screen for all neuromodulation
Notes: Covers: implants, seizure history, medications, pregnancy, cardiac, skin integrity.

Use_Case: Baseline brain activity mapping for protocol selection
License_or_Access_Notes: Requires qEEG equipment and normative database (proprietary)
Scoring_Type: Frequency band power; coherence; asymmetry metrics
Notes: qEEG-guided treatment selection is clinician-facing only. Not a diagnostic tool.

Use_Case: Tinnitus severity and impact assessment
Related_Phenotypes: PHE-016 (note: different from OCD phenotype — tinnitus-specific)
Notes: Grade 1 (slight, 0-16) to Grade 5 (catastrophic, 78-100).

Use_Case: Treatment-responsive tinnitus outcome measure
License_or_Access_Notes: Free for clinical and research use
Notes: More treatment-responsive than THI. 13-point change = clinically meaningful.

Use_Case: Autism trait screening
License_or_Access_Notes: Free for research and clinical use (ARC Cambridge)
Notes: Screening only, not diagnostic. Cutoff ≥32 suggests possible ASD.

Use_Case: Social communication/interaction assessment
Notes: Dimensional measure of ASD traits. Used in neuromodulation ASD research.

Use_Case: Nicotine dependence severity for smoking cessation TMS
Notes: Score ≥6 indicates high dependence.

Use_Case: Opioid withdrawal severity tracking for taVNS
Notes: Mild (5-12), Moderate (13-24), Moderately severe (25-36), Severe (≥37).

Use_Case: Upper extremity motor recovery tracking post-stroke
Notes: Gold standard for post-stroke motor recovery. Used in rTMS stroke trials.; Public domain — no single authoritative URL

Use_Case: PD motor symptom tracking for DBS
Scoring_Type: Multiple parts; Part III motor exam most used
Notes: MDS-UPDRS Part III used to track DBS motor response. 40-60% improvement typical.

Use_Case: Seizure frequency/severity tracking for VNS/DBS
Notes: Essential for VNS and DBS efficacy monitoring. Track: type, duration, triggers, post-ictal state.
